content.what: LEO (Living Ecosystem Orchestrator) — a multi-agent system core that manages all the owner's projects. This repo is not a product, it's the definition of the working method that downstream projects (Logbook, Saintfy, future ones) inherit. The KB is a neural network of JSON docs connected by tags, consumed by agents, maintained by scripts.
stack: JSON Knowledge Base (.claude/kb/) with schema validation
Bash scripts for zero-token deterministic work (validate, index, stale detection)
Node.js + TypeScript CLI (cli/) with @clack/prompts and commander
Markdown for platform-constrained files only (CLAUDE.md bootloader, agent definitions)
Claude Code hooks for automated KB maintenance
content.philosophy: Copilot-style, not Paperclip-style. Owner decides the what and why, Leo decides the who (routing and delegation), Managers decide the how. Token Economy: AI spends tokens on thinking and judgment, scripts handle deterministic work at zero cost. Knowledge is JSON for agents, human-readable outputs are generated on demand. Extend, never override.
constraints: All KB content is JSON — MD only exists as platform constraints (CLAUDE.md, agent files)
Core artifacts are English only — interaction language is personal choice (gitignored)
No rule or Manager change without explicit R2 from the owner
Specialists live in projects, never in the core — core keeps only universal managers
Scripts must never require AI tokens — if it's deterministic, it's a script
